heading: Nambucca Heads vs Nana Glen — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Nambucca Heads, New South Wales and Nana Glen, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Nana Glen has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to Nambucca Heads (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Nambucca Heads or Nana Glen safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Nambucca Heads has a flood risk score of 23/100, while Nana Glen scores 26/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Nambucca Heads scores 54/100 for bushfire risk. Nana Glen scores 48/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Nambucca Heads or Nana Glen?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Nana Glen has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.55% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
